blocks.content: About four months in, he went to the studio and opened a sketchbook.
Not a blank one. He reached and it was there, and he opened it without looking at the cover first.

The drawing of her asleep on the couch. Years ago — an ordinary Wednesday, the October light coming through the window, her hand half-open against the cushion. He had drawn it slowly, the way you draw something you want to keep.
He sat with the sketchbook open in his lap for a long time.
He looked at the line of her. The specific quality of the light. The way he had captured the exact shape of the particular peace she had in sleep.
He drew one line on a blank page. Just one line — the beginning of a shape he couldn't name yet.
He came back the next day.
